note: Engine line coverage ratchet. Gated buckets may not drop more than maxDropPct below `measured`. Measured over the HONEST combined surface: the unit lcov UNIONed with the Phase C in-process harness lcov (scripts/merge-coverage.ts). See docs/HARDENING-9-PLAN.md H-02.
targetNote:
target.lib=90 is the autonomous coverage campaign goal for the lib/ business-logic core. The gate measures the HONEST combined number: unit lcov UNIONed with the in-process harness lcov (scripts/merge-coverage.ts). Harness route suites (src/tests/harness/) plus focused unit tests climb toward 90%. External-service wiring (better-auth, S3, pg-boss, ioredis, Bun.SQL.subscribe, OTel SDK) + defensive catch-branches remain for integration/soak suites.

2026-07-15: measured.lib jumped 88.8 -> 96.7 WITHOUT new tests. This is not inflation. merge-coverage.ts stopped counting non-executable lines: bun's lcov emits DA records for // comments and blank lines and scores them hits=0, which across src/ charged 1663 comment + 1476 blank lines (92 of 157 files) to the denominator. Hit counts were unaffected; only the denominator dropped. The number now means what it says -- percent of EXECUTABLE lib lines covered. target.lib stays 90: it is met with real headroom, and the 0.5pt ratchet is what guards erosion from here.
source: union of `bun test src/tests/unit --coverage` and `bun test src/tests/harness --coverage` lcovs via scripts/merge-coverage.ts (per-line DA hit union, non-executable lines excluded)
routesNote: 2026-07-20: `routes` joined the RATCHET (gated) but deliberately has NO entry in `target`. Rationale: every authz bug in this project's history landed in routes/ -- the 9 fixed in the security audit, the tenant-isolation campaign, the storage IDOR -- yet the layer with the highest historical bug incidence was the one bucket nothing guarded, free to erode. Gating it pins routes at its current 59.7% via maxDropPct, which is the actual goal (stop erosion where bugs happen), and costs nothing today. It has no `target` on purpose: release-gate.ts skips buckets without one, so this does NOT introduce a new 3.0.0 blocker. Raising it to a real bar (65 was proposed) is an OWNER call, because that WOULD block the cut until routes coverage is written.
